free_text: Proton-90Zr interaction at sub-Coulomb proton energies
Eastern Kentucky University, Richmond,
Kentucky
The University of Kentucky 6.5 MeV HVEC
Van de Graaff accelerator.
131 cm3 or an 80 cm3 Ge(Li) detector for
proton energies from 1.9 to 5.7 MeV.
The target foils were made by
rolling zirconium metal. The target thicknesses and
uniformity were determined from the elastic proton
scattering at energies of 2 MeV where the scattering
is predominantly Coulomb. The thicknesses were
checked by accurately measuring the weight and area
of the target foils, and the two methods agreed to
within 2%. The resulting thicknesses of the two
targets used in the experiments were 439+-9 and
885+-53 microg/cm2.
For gamma-ray measurements, the targets
were mounted in a glass chamber and electrically
connected to the Faraday cup and beam stop. Aluminum
gamma-ray absorbers with a total thickness of 1.34
g/cm2 were placed between the target and the detector
to prevent pulse summing and to reduce low-energy
signals which degrade the resolution of the detector.
The Ge(Li) detector was located at an angle of 125
degrees to the beam direction and at a distance of
approximately 7 cm from the target. The angle of 125,
a zero of P2(cos theta), made it possible to deduce
the total cross section from the measured
differential cross section, assuming that terms of
order higher than P2 were small.
Approved by C.E. Laird on July 25, 2017.
